revision.text:
'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭り]]のこと。
*'''[[神田祭]]''' :[[神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 *'''[[山王祭]]''' :[[日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。
残りの一つに対しては諸説がある。 *[[深川祭]] :[[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。 *[[三社祭]] :[[浅草神社]]で行われる祭り。 *[[根津神社例祭]] :[[根津神社]]で行われる祭り。 を入れるのが一般的。
{{DEFAULTSORT:えとさんたいまつり}} [[Category:東京都の祭り|*さんたいまつり]] [[Category:名数3]] {{event-stub}}

revision.comment: 江戸三大祭りの３つ目がどの祭であるかの議論について補足（三社祭の追加）。
revision.text:
'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭り]]のこと。
次の2つがいわば江戸幕府公認の江戸を代表する祭りであることは異論がない。 {{cite web | url = http://www.to-con.com/FRaU/017/index.php | title = Tokyo Concierge #17 江戸三大祭り | accessdate = 2012-3-18 | author = 小平雅一 | date = 2002-5-5 | work = 東京コンシェルジェ | publisher = TOKYO CONCIERGE PROJECT }} * '''[[神田祭]]''' : [[神田明神]]で行われる[[祭り]]。 * '''[[山王祭]]''' : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる[[祭り]]。 一方、３つ目の大祭としては以下の２つのいずれを加えるかで議論があるが結論が出ていない、あるいはどちらでもよいというのが現状のようである。

revision.comment: 三社公式に明記はなし。他3神社には明記あり。
revision.text:

「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。 *'''[[神田祭]]''' :[[神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 *'''[[山王祭]]''' :[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 *'''[[深川祭]]''' :[[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。

'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭|祭り]]のこと。
「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。だが、深川祭を外して、[[浅草神社]]の[[三社祭]]を入れる場合もある<ref>[http://karyukai-geisya.jimdo.com/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E3%81%AE%E4%BC%9D%E7%B5%B1/%E4%B8%89%E7%A4%BE%E7%A5%AD-%E6%B1%9F%E6%88%B8%E4%B8%89%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%8A%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%80%E3%81%A4/ 三社祭（江戸三大祭りの一つ）]</ref>。 *'''[[神田祭]]''' :[[神田明神|神田神社]]で行われる祭り。 *'''[[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]]''' :[[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 *'''[[深川祭]]''' :[[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。

「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。[[江戸時代]]から伝わる年中行事である。
* '''[[神田祭]]''' : [[神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]]''' : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[深川祭]]''' : [[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。
== 関連項目 == * [[特別:Prefixindex/江戸三大|江戸三大で始まる記事の一覧]] * [[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]] - 3か所共に[[江戸]]象徴の中心地である日本橋地域をそれぞれ[[氏子]]に持つ。 * [[東京十社]] - [[東京都区部|東京特別区]]内の社格が高い10社を示し、3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[別表神社]] - 3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[天下祭]] - 江戸時代には、二大祭（神田祭・山王祭）の[[山車]]が[[江戸城]]に入り、[[将軍]]が拝したことから両祭を天下祭または御用祭と称した。
{{DEFAULTSORT:えとさんたいまつり}} [[Category:東京都の祭り|*さんたいまつり]] [[Category:三大祭り]] {{event-stub}}

'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは、[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭|祭り]]のこと。
「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。[[江戸時代]]から伝わる年中行事である。
* '''[[神田祭]]''' : [[神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]]''' : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 神田・山王は定説だが、三つ目は深川の他に浅草三社・根津など諸説ある。
== 関連項目 == * [[特別:Prefixindex/江戸三大|江戸三大で始まる記事の一覧]] * [[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]] - 3か所共に[[江戸]]象徴の中心地である日本橋地域をそれぞれ[[氏子]]に持つ。 * [[東京十社]] - [[東京都区部|東京特別区]]内の社格が高い10社を示し、3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[別表神社]] - 3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[天下祭]] - 江戸時代には、二大祭（神田祭・山王祭）の[[山車]]が[[江戸城]]に入り、[[将軍]]が拝したことから両祭を天下祭または御用祭と称した。

revision.text:
'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは、[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭|祭り]]のこと。
「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。[[江戸時代]]から伝わる年中行事である。
* '''[[神田祭]]''' : [[神田神社|神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]]''' : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[深川祭]]''' : [[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。
== 関連項目 == * [[特別:Prefixindex/江戸三大|江戸三大で始まる記事の一覧]] * [[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]] - 3か所共に[[江戸]]象徴の中心地である日本橋地域をそれぞれ[[氏子]]に持つ。 * [[東京十社]] - [[東京都区部|東京特別区]]内の社格が高い10社を示し、3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[別表神社]] - 3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[天下祭]] - 江戸時代には、二大祭（神田祭・山王祭）の[[山車]]が[[江戸城]]に入り、[[将軍]]が拝したことから両祭を天下祭または御用祭と称した。
{{DEFAULTSORT:えとさんたいまつり}} [[Category:東京都の祭り|*さんたいまつり]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:麹町]] [[Category:神田]] [[Category:日本橋 (東京都中央区)]] [[Category:深川]] {{event-stub}}

revision.text:
'''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは、[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭|祭り]]のこと。
「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様。」と表した。[[江戸時代]]から伝わる年中行事である。
* '''[[神田祭]]''' : [[神田明神]]で行われる祭り。 * '''[[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]]''' : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 神田・山王は定説だが、三つ目は深川の他に浅草三社・根津など諸説ある。
== 関連項目 == * [[特別:Prefixindex/江戸三大|江戸三大で始まる記事の一覧]] * [[日本橋 (東京都中央区)|日本橋]] - 3か所共に[[江戸]]象徴の中心地である日本橋地域をそれぞれ[[氏子]]に持つ。 * [[東京十社]] - [[東京都区部|東京特別区]]内の社格が高い10社を示し、3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[別表神社]] - 3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[天下祭]] - 江戸時代には、二大祭（神田祭・山王祭）の[[山車]]が[[江戸城]]に入り、[[将軍]]が拝したことから両祭を天下祭または御用祭と称した。
{{DEFAULTSORT:えとさんたいまつり}} [[Category:東京都の祭り|*さんたいまつり]] [[Category:三大祭り]] {{event-stub}}

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/221.16.38.59|221.16.38.59]] ([[User talk:221.16.38.59|会話]]) による「三社・根津など諸説ある」の根拠なし。公式に明記もなし。深川含む3神社には明記あり。)

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Otheruses}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}} {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り}} held in {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦}}'s seventh month are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon D..."

revision.text:
{{Otheruses]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014]] {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り]] refne of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Edo|en}}[[ja:江戸三大祭り]].
* {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en}}[[ja:石川県]] ** {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en}}[[ja:飯田町燈籠山祭り]] * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en}}[[ja:岐阜県]] ** {{ill|Gujo Odori|en}}[[ja:郡上踊り]] * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}}[[ja:愛知県]] ** {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en}}[[ja:尾張津島天王祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en}}[[ja:日本三大川祭]]. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en}}[[ja:京都府]] ** {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}}[[ja:祇園祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en}}[[ja:日本三大祭り]]. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:大阪府]] ** {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en}}[[ja:天神祭]] – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]]. * {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en}}[[ja:兵庫県]] ** {{ill|Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|en}}[[ja:湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント]] * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en}}[[ja:鳥取県]] ** {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en}}[[ja:鳥取しゃんしゃん祭]] * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en}}[[ja:徳島県]] ** {{ill|Awa Odori|en}}[[ja:阿波踊り]] – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en}}[[ja:徳島市阿波おどり]] is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en}}[[ja:四国三大祭り]]. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en}}[[ja:高知県]] ** {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|en}}[[ja:よさこい祭り]] – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[ja:福岡県]] ** {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}[[ja:博多祇園山笠]] * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en}}[[ja:熊本県]] ** {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}[[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]] * {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en}}[[ja:宮崎県]] ** {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}[[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]]
== Summer Festivals Outside Japan == * {{ill|Saban Tuy|en}}[[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}[[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}[[ja:ロシア]].

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at {{ill|Kanda Shrine (Kanda Myojin)|ja|神田明神}}. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chiyoda)|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at {{ill|Tomioka Hachimangu|ja|富岡八幡宮}}. == Related Topics == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Bekkaku Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{Otheruses]] The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at {{ill|Kanda Shrine (Kanda Myojin)|ja|神田明神}}. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chiyoda)|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa special wards. * {{ill|Bekkaku Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Bekkaku Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:江戸三大祭り]] * {{ill|Ishikawa {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:江戸三大祭り]] * {{ill|Ishikawa {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:江戸三大祭り]] * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:石川県]] ** {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:飯田町燈籠山祭り]] * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:岐阜県]] ** One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本三大川祭]]. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:京都府]] ** {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:祇園祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本三大祭り]]. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]] ** {{ill|Tenjin （2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:江戸三大祭り]] * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:石川県]] ** {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:飯田町燈籠山祭り]] * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:岐阜県]] ** {{ill|Gujo Odori|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:郡上踊り]] * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:愛知県]] ** {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:尾張津島天王祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本三大川祭]]. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:京都府]] ** {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:祇園祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日本三大祭り]]. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]] ** {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天神祭]] – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]]. * {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:兵庫県]] ** {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント]] * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鳥取県]] ** {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鳥取しゃんしゃん祭]] * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:徳島県]] ** {{ill|Awa Odori|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿波踊り]] – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:徳島市阿波おどり]] is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:四国三大祭り]]. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高知県]] ** {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:よさこい祭り]] – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:福岡県]] ** {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:博多祇園山笠]] * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:熊本県]] ** {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]] * {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮崎県]] ** {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]] * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ロシア]]. * {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: **"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."** These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[ja:江戸三大祭り]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three revision.text:
{{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Categoery:qq]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category: :qq]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category: :qq]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Fukagawa]] The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines
