{{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] （2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:Kojimachi]] [[Category:Kanda]] [[Category:Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. （2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 3]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]][[sn:Three Great Festivals of Edo]] == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. {{DEFAULTSORT:Edo San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:Festivals in Tokyo|*San-Dai Matsuri]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Three Great Festivals]] {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}. * {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Awa Odori|en}} – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en}} is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en}}. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|en}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}} * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}} * {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}} * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}} – A summer festival held along the middlereaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}} in {{ill|Russia|en}}. * {{ill|Spring {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る

（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)|12=draft}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=draft}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=draft}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=draft}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}. * The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)|12=draft|12=draft}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=draft|12=draft}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - All three shrines hold this {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009年5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010年6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009年8月16日撮影）]] }} '''江戸三大祭り'''（えどさんだいまつり）とは、[[東京都]]内で行われる3つの大きな[[祭|祭り]]のこと。 「神輿深川、山車神田、だだっ広いが山王様」と表した。[[江戸時代]]から伝わる年中行事である。 ; [[神田祭]] : [[神田明神|神田神社]]で行われる祭り。 ; [[山王祭 (千代田区)|山王祭]] : [[日枝神社 (千代田区)|日枝神社]]で行われる祭り。 ; [[深川祭]] : [[富岡八幡宮]]で行われる祭り。 == 関連項目 == * {{Prefix|江戸三大}} * [[日本橋 (東京都中央区)]] - 3か所共に[[江戸]]象徴の中心地である日本橋地域をそれぞれ[[氏子]]に持つ。 * [[東京十社]] - [[東京都区部|東京特別区]]内の社格が高い10社を示し、3社共に指定を受けている。 * [[別表神社]] - 3社共に指定を受けている。 {{DEFAULTSORT:えとさんたいまつり}} [[Category:東京都の祭り|*さんたいまつり]] [[Category:江戸幕府]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:麹町]] [[Category:神田]] [[Category:日本橋 (東京都中央区)]] [[Category:深川]]

The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine|qq=}}. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' （2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Tottori （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * revision.text:
{{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yumura （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the （2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; [[:en:Kanda Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by [[:en:Hie Shrine|Hie Shrine]]. ; [[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]] : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] [[ja:江戸三大祭り]][[zh:江戶三大祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11550984]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Edo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{右| [[File:Kanda Matsuri1.jpg|thumb|none|神田祭 神幸祭 江戸の下町を巡行する
（2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; {{ill|Kanda Matsuri|en|Kanda Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri}} : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine}}. ; {{ill|Fukagawa Matsuri|en|Fukagawa Matsuri|WD=Q3090688|lt=Fukagawa Matsuri}} : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within their {{ill|parishioners|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=ujigami|WD=Q767655}}. * {{ill|Tokyo Ten Shrines|ja|東京十社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Ten Shrines|qq=}} - These three shrines are part of the "Tokyo Ten Shrines," a designation for the top-Ranking shrines in Tokyo's 23 special wards. * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} - All three shrines hold this designation. * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gujo Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gujo Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Matsuri|qq=}} – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Fire Festival|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|ja|湯村温泉 (兵庫県)#イベント|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8061163}} * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Awa Odori|qq=}} is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumamoto （2009th year 5月9日撮影）]] [[File:Hie jinjya-7.jpg|thumb|none|山王祭 神輿宮入 山王鳥居から表参道（山王男坂）を神輿が昇る
（2010th year 6月12日撮影）]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|none|深川祭 本祭 御本社二の宮神輿（重量約2トン）
（2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; {{ill|Kanda Matsuri|en|Kanda Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri}} : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine}}. ; {{ill|Fukagawa Matsuri|en|Fukagawa Matsuri|WD=Q3090688|lt=Fukagawa Matsuri}} : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the Nihonbashi area, a symbolic center of Edo, within （2009th year 8月16日撮影）]] }} '''Edo San-Dai Matsuri''' (江戸三大祭り) refers to the three major traditional festivals held in Tokyo, Japan. The festivals are described with the phrase: '''"Mikoshi in Fukagawa, floats in Kanda, and spacious grounds for Sanno-sama."''' These festivals have been celebrated as annual events since the Edo period. ; {{ill|Kanda Matsuri|en|Kanda Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri|1=Kanda Matsuri}} : Held at [[Kanda Shrine]]. ; [[Sanno Matsuri (Chiyoda-ku)|Sanno Matsuri]] : Hosted by {{ill|Hie Shrine|en|Hie Shrine|WD=Q702042|lt=Hie Shrine|1=Hie Shrine}}. ; {{ill|Fukagawa Matsuri|en|Fukagawa Matsuri|WD=Q3090688|lt=Fukagawa Matsuri|1=Fukagawa Matsuri}} : Celebrated at [[Tomioka Hachiman Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{Prefix|Edo San-Dai}} * [[Nihonbashi (Chuo-ku, Tokyo)|Nihonbashi]] - All three shrines include the
